articles.headline: India Unveils New Policy Framework to Accelerate Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) announced a comprehensive policy framework offering production-linked incentives (PLI) and viability gap funding (VGF) for green hydrogen and electrolyser manufacturing.
• The policy aims to reduce the cost of green hydrogen production, making it competitive with fossil-fuel-based hydrogen, and promote India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
• Key components include tax breaks for early adopters, dedicated green hydrogen corridors, and R&D support for advanced electrolyser technologies.
• The move is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and achieving Net Zero emissions by 2070.
• Industry experts laud the policy for providing long-term visibility and investor confidence in the nascent green hydrogen sector.

Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
New and renewable energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
MNRE is the nodal ministry.

mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy for energy security and decarbonization through green hydrogen.

Evaluate the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.

articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces 'Right to be Forgotten' in Digital Age, Calls for Comprehensive Data Protection Law
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment emphasizing the 'Right to be Forgotten' as an intrinsic part of the Right to Privacy under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling came in a case involving the removal of outdated personal information from public digital platforms that was causing reputational harm to an individual.
• The Court urged the government to expedite the enactment of a robust, comprehensive data protection law that addresses emerging challenges of the digital era, including data localization and cross-border data flows.
• It highlighted the need for a balance between an individual's right to privacy and the public's right to information, especially concerning public figures.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for internet intermediaries, search engines, and social media platforms operating in India.

Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

prelimsPoints: Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared Right to Privacy a fundamental right.
Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee report on data protection.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees protection of life and personal liberty.
The Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019 (now Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023) is relevant.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Right to Privacy in India and its implications for digital governance.

articles.trivia: The concept of 'Right to be Forgotten' originated in Europe, with a landmark ruling by the European Court of Justice in 2014.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Dialogue Focuses on Enhanced Maritime Security and Regional Stability
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the annual India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue, emphasizing cooperation on traditional and non-traditional security threats in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on joint naval exercises, information sharing, capacity building for coastal surveillance, and combating piracy and illegal fishing.
• Both sides reiterated commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, upholding international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• The dialogue also touched upon humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) operations and enhancing interoperability among maritime agencies.
• This engagement underscores India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic convergence with ASEAN nations.

Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.

articles.trivia: The Strait of Malacca, a crucial maritime chokepoint, connects the Indian Ocean to the Pacific Ocean, making maritime security in the region vital for global trade.

articles.headline: RBI Expands Digital Rupee (e₹) Pilot to Include More Banks and Use Cases
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced a significant expansion of its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot project for both retail (e₹-R) and wholesale (e₹-W) segments.
• The expansion includes onboarding more public and private sector banks and exploring new use cases beyond interbank settlements and person-to-person transactions.
• Focus areas for new use cases include programmable payments, offline functionality, and integration with existing payment infrastructure like UPI.
• The RBI aims to gather more data on user experience, technological robustness, and economic impact before a full-scale rollout.
• This move signals India's proactive approach to digital currencies and financial innovation, potentially revolutionizing the payment landscape.

articles.headline: ISRO Achieves Key Milestone in Next-Gen Launch Vehicle Development
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a critical hot test of the cryogenic upper stage engine for its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV).
• The NGLV is envisioned as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle capable of carrying heavier payloads into various orbits.
• This test validates the indigenous design and manufacturing capabilities for advanced propulsion systems, crucial for future space missions.
• The NGLV aims to replace existing operational launchers like PSLV and GSLV, offering enhanced payload capacity and reduced launch costs through reusability.
• This development is vital for India's ambitions in commercial space launches, human spaceflight (Gaganyaan), and deep-space exploration.

Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: PSLV is ISRO's workhorse launch vehicle.

mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing indigenous heavy-lift and reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program.
Analyze how NGLV can enhance India's capabilities in commercial space, human spaceflight, and deep-space missions.

Evaluate the role of ISRO in promoting scientific temper and technological self-reliance in India.

articles.trivia: The first successful flight of a cryogenic engine by ISRO was in 2014 with the GSLV-D5 mission.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level meeting to review the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states and Union Territories.
• Discussions focused on the progress in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, digital learning initiatives, and teacher training programs.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, faculty shortages, and the need for greater state-level coordination in adopting the new framework.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to achieving the policy's goals, particularly universal access to quality education and skill development.
• Emphasis was placed on leveraging technology for education delivery and assessment, in line with the policy's vision.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Strengthen South-South Cooperation on Climate Action and Renewable Energy
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil held high-level bilateral talks, reiterating their commitment to strengthening South-South cooperation, particularly in climate change mitigation and renewable energy.
• Discussions included joint research and development projects in biofuels, solar energy, and sustainable agriculture.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of a common position in international climate negotiations, advocating for climate justice and equitable burden-sharing.
• They agreed to share best practices in sustainable development models and collaborate on technology transfer for green technologies.
• This partnership highlights the growing role of major developing economies in global environmental governance.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches New Wetland Conservation and Management Plan
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) unveiled an enhanced national action plan for the conservation and sustainable management of wetlands.
• The plan focuses on holistic approaches, including community participation, scientific inventorying of wetlands, restoration of degraded sites, and stricter enforcement against encroachment.
• It integrates climate change resilience measures and promotes eco-tourism opportunities around identified wetlands.
• Special emphasis is laid on wetlands outside the Ramsar List, ensuring broader protection for diverse aquatic ecosystems.
• This initiative is critical for biodiversity preservation, water security, and mitigating the impacts of climate change.

Disaster and disaster management.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Releases New 'Positive Indigenisation List' for Defence Procurement
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence released its fourth 'Positive Indigenisation List' (PIL) comprising 101 items that will be procured only from domestic sources after specific timelines.
• This list includes complex systems, sub-systems, and components, further boosting India's self-reliance in defence manufacturing.
• The move is aimed at reducing import dependency, stimulating domestic R&D, and strengthening the 'Make in India' initiative in the defence sector.
• The policy provides a clear roadmap for the Indian defence industry to invest in technological upgrades and production capabilities.
• It is expected to create significant opportunities for MSMEs and startups in the defence ecosystem.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for UN Reforms at General Assembly Session
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN addressed the 79th session of the UN General Assembly, reiterating India's long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• The address highlighted the anachronistic nature of the current UNSC structure, which does not reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• India emphasized the need for greater representation for developing countries and increased transparency in the Council's functioning.
• The statement also touched upon global challenges such as climate change, terrorism, and sustainable development, urging collective action.
• This consistent advocacy underscores India's aspiration for a permanent seat in a reformed UNSC.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Milestone in Health ID Generation
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced a significant milestone, surpassing 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA) IDs generated nationwide.
• This achievement marks substantial progress in creating a seamless online platform for healthcare services, enabling digital storage and access to medical records.
• The mission aims to build the necessary infrastructure to support India's integrated digital health ecosystem, connecting patients, doctors, and other healthcare providers.
• It facilitates interoperability of health data and promotes paperless healthcare, improving efficiency and accessibility.
• The government plans further outreach to ensure wider adoption, especially in rural and remote areas, to realize the vision of 'Health for All'.

articles.headline: UNEP Report Highlights Persistent Global Fossil Fuel Subsidies Despite Climate Goals
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the UN Environment Programme (UNEP) revealed that global fossil fuel subsidies continue to rise, undermining efforts to combat climate change.
• The report indicates that governments worldwide spent record amounts on supporting fossil fuels in 2023, largely due to energy price volatility and cost-of-living crises.
• It calls for an urgent phase-out of inefficient fossil fuel subsidies and a redirection of funds towards renewable energy and sustainable alternatives.
• The persistence of these subsidies creates market distortions, discourages green investments, and exacerbates greenhouse gas emissions.
• This finding poses a significant challenge for countries committed to their Paris Agreement targets and achieving global decarbonization.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: UNEP is the leading global environmental authority.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Shows Strong Recovery, Focus on Sustainable Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism reported a significant rebound in both domestic and international tourist arrivals, surpassing pre-pandemic levels in several key destinations.
• The recovery is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh', improved connectivity, and targeted marketing campaigns.
• There's a growing emphasis on promoting sustainable and responsible tourism, including eco-tourism, rural tourism, and cultural heritage circuits.
• New policies are being formulated to enhance infrastructure, ensure tourist safety, and develop lesser-known destinations.
• The sector is poised for further growth, contributing significantly to employment generation and foreign exchange earnings.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Ethical AI Governance Framework at Global AI Summit
articles.explainer:
• India participated in a global Artificial Intelligence (AI) summit, advocating for a multilateral governance framework that ensures ethical, responsible, and inclusive development and deployment of AI.
• The Indian delegation highlighted the need for a human-centric approach to AI, addressing concerns around bias, privacy, accountability, and the socio-economic impact of automation.
• India emphasized its commitment to leveraging AI for public good, citing initiatives in healthcare, agriculture, and education.
• The discussions also focused on international cooperation for AI research, standard-setting, and capacity building, especially for developing nations.
• This stance aligns with India's vision of 'AI for All' and its growing leadership in digital public infrastructure.

articles.headline: Monsoon Withdrawal Progresses, Mixed Impact on Kharif Harvest and Rabi Sowing
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) announced the further progression of monsoon withdrawal across parts of Northwest and Central India.
• While overall monsoon rainfall for the season was near normal, regional disparities have led to mixed outcomes for the ongoing Kharif crop harvest.
• Adequate soil moisture in some regions bodes well for the upcoming Rabi sowing, particularly for wheat and pulses.
• However, concerns remain for areas that experienced deficit rainfall, potentially impacting agricultural yields and necessitating irrigation support.
• The government is monitoring the situation closely and preparing contingency plans for affected farmers, including support for Rabi crop diversification.

articles.headline: Editorial: Towards Resilient Urban Planning for Climate Change Adaptation
articles.explainer:

• It emphasizes the need for integrated strategies that incorporate climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction into city development plans.
• Key recommendations include investing in green infrastructure, improving drainage systems, promoting sustainable transport, and strengthening early warning mechanisms.
• The editorial argues for greater collaboration between urban local bodies, state governments, and citizens in building climate-resilient cities.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Uncover New Insights at Ancient Harappan Site in Haryana
articles.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists from the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced significant new findings at a Harappan site in Haryana.
• The discoveries include evidence of advanced urban planning, water management systems, and unique pottery artifacts, shedding new light on the mature Harappan period.
• Further excavations revealed distinct residential and industrial zones, suggesting a highly organized society with specialized craftsmanship.
• Analysis of unearthed seals and figurines provides clues about trade networks and religious practices of the ancient civilization.
• These findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization's spread and cultural nuances beyond well-known sites like Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa.

articles.headline: Global Hunger Index 2024: India's Position and Efforts to Combat Malnutrition
articles.explainer:
• The Global Hunger Index (GHI) 2024 report was released, indicating India's continued progress in addressing hunger and malnutrition, though challenges persist.
• India's ranking improved slightly, reflecting ongoing efforts through various government schemes targeting food security and nutritional outcomes.
• The report highlighted specific areas where India needs to intensify efforts, such as reducing child wasting and improving dietary diversity.
• Government officials reiterated their commitment to achieving Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 2: Zero Hunger by 2030.
• The GHI report serves as a critical tool for assessing national and global progress in combating hunger and identifying areas for intervention.

articles.headline: New Study Highlights Unique Biodiversity and Threats in Eastern Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A recent ecological study published in a leading journal underscored the unique biodiversity of the Eastern Ghats, identifying several endemic species of flora and fauna.
• The study warned of increasing threats to this mountain range, including deforestation, mining, agricultural expansion, and climate change.
• Researchers emphasized the critical role of the Eastern Ghats as a crucial ecological corridor and a biodiversity hotspot, often overshadowed by the Western Ghats.
• It called for urgent conservation efforts, including designating new protected areas and implementing sustainable land-use practices.
• The findings are vital for formulating targeted conservation policies and promoting ecological restoration in the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.

They are older than the Western Ghats.
They receive rainfall from the Northeast Monsoon.
Endemic species are found exclusively in a particular geographical area.

mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Eastern Ghats and compare its biodiversity with the Western Ghats.
Analyze the major threats to the biodiversity of the Eastern Ghats and their long-term environmental implications.
Examine the role of scientific research in informing conservation strategies and policy decisions.

articles.headline: India's Startup Ecosystem Continues Robust Growth Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• A new report by industry body NASSCOM highlighted the continued robust growth of India's startup ecosystem, with a significant increase in new ventures and funding.
• Despite global economic headwinds, India remains a top destination for venture capital, particularly in sectors like FinTech, HealthTech, and DeepTech.
• Government initiatives such as 'Startup India' and 'Fund of Funds for Startups' are credited for fostering a conducive environment for innovation.
• The report also pointed to the increasing number of unicorns and the expansion of startup activity beyond metropolitan areas to Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
• This growth is crucial for job creation, technological innovation, and India's aspiration to become a $5 trillion economy.

Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Frog in Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The newly identified amphibian, named 'Nyctibatrachus sahyadriensis', belongs to the genus of night frogs, known for their unique breeding habits.
• This discovery underscores the unparalleled biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect the fragile habitats of such endemic species from climate change and human encroachment.
• The finding contributes significantly to the understanding of amphibian evolution and distribution in the Indian subcontinent.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues Advisory on Rising Cyber Threats and Data Breaches
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) issued a comprehensive advisory warning against a surge in cybersecurity threats, including ransomware attacks and data breaches.
• The advisory urged government agencies, critical infrastructure operators, and private organizations to bolster their digital defenses and implement robust cybersecurity protocols.
• It highlighted the growing sophistication of cyber adversaries and the increasing targeting of personal data and financial systems.
• CERT-In recommended regular security audits, employee training, and timely patching of vulnerabilities to mitigate risks.
• This alert underscores the critical importance of national cybersecurity strategies in safeguarding digital assets and citizen data.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The Ministry of Power is the nodal ministry for its implementation.
3. Production Linked Incentives (PLI) and Viability Gap Funding (VGF) are key financial mechanisms under the mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission's overarching goal is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production, utilization, and export. Statement 2 is incorrect. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry for the National Green Hydrogen Mission. Statement 3 is correct. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme, a component of the mission, provides financial incentives for manufacturing electrolysers and producing green hydrogen through PLI and VGF mechanisms.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for issuing advisories and handling cybersecurity incidents in India?
options: (d) National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency established to deal with cyber security incidents. It collects, analyzes, and disseminates information on cyber incidents, and issues forecasts and alerts of cyber security threats.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India:
1. It is explicitly enshrined as a separate fundamental right in the Indian Constitution.
2. The Supreme Court has recognized it as an intrinsic part of the Right to Privacy under Article 21.
3. The Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019, provided for the 'Right to Erasure' which is similar in concept.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The 'Right to be Forgotten' is not explicitly enshrined as a separate fundamental right but has been interpreted by the judiciary. Statement 2 is correct. The Supreme Court, building on the Puttaswamy judgment, has recognized it as an intrinsic part of the Right to Privacy under Article 21. Statement 3 is correct. The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (previously Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019) includes provisions for the 'Right to Erasure' or correction of personal data, which is analogous to the 'Right to be Forgotten'.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following indicators are used to calculate the Global Hunger Index (GHI)?
1. Undernourishment
2. Child Wasting
3. Child Stunting
4. Child Mortality
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Global Hunger Index (GHI) is a tool that measures and tracks hunger globally, regionally, and nationally. The GHI score is calculated based on four indicators: 1. Undernourishment (proportion of the population with insufficient caloric intake), 2. Child Wasting (proportion of children under the age of five who have low weight for their height, reflecting acute undernutrition), 3. Child Stunting (proportion of children under the age of five who have low height for their age, reflecting chronic undernutrition), and 4. Child Mortality (mortality rate of children under the age of five).

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to India's space program, what is the primary objective of the Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV)?
options: (a) To serve as a dedicated launch vehicle for small satellites only.
(b) To replace PSLV for all future ISRO missions without reusability features.
(c) To develop a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle for various orbits.
(d) To exclusively launch human spaceflight missions to the Moon.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To develop a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle for various orbits.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) is envisioned by ISRO as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle capable of carrying heavier payloads into Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO), Low Earth Orbit (LEO), and other orbits. It aims to reduce launch costs and enhance payload capacity, serving commercial, human spaceflight (Gaganyaan), and deep-space missions. Options (a), (b), and (d) are incorrect as they either limit its scope or misrepresent its core features like reusability and heavy-lift capability.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic importance of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving energy security and climate goals. What are the key challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and how can the new policy framework address them? (15 marks, 250 words)

GS1.headline: Monsoon Withdrawal Progresses, Mixed Impact on Kharif Harvest and Rabi Sowing
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) announced the further progression of monsoon withdrawal across parts of Northwest and Central India.
• While overall monsoon rainfall for the season was near normal, regional disparities have led to mixed outcomes for the ongoing Kharif crop harvest.
• Adequate soil moisture in some regions bodes well for the upcoming Rabi sowing, particularly for wheat and pulses.
• However, concerns remain for areas that experienced deficit rainfall, potentially impacting agricultural yields and necessitating irrigation support.
• The government is monitoring the situation closely and preparing contingency plans for affected farmers, including support for Rabi crop diversification.

prelimsPoints: Monsoon withdrawal typically begins in September from Northwest India.
Kharif crops are sown in monsoon and harvested in autumn (e.g., rice, maize).
Rabi crops are sown in winter and harvested in spring (e.g., wheat, barley).
IMD is the primary agency for meteorological observations and forecasting.
El Niño generally correlates with weaker monsoons in India.

GS1.headline: Archaeologists Uncover New Insights at Ancient Harappan Site in Haryana
GS1.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists from the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced significant new findings at a Harappan site in Haryana.
• The discoveries include evidence of advanced urban planning, water management systems, and unique pottery artifacts, shedding new light on the mature Harappan period.
• Further excavations revealed distinct residential and industrial zones, suggesting a highly organized society with specialized craftsmanship.
• Analysis of unearthed seals and figurines provides clues about trade networks and religious practices of the ancient civilization.
• These findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization's spread and cultural nuances beyond well-known sites like Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces 'Right to be Forgotten' in Digital Age, Calls for Comprehensive Data Protection Law
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment emphasizing the 'Right to be Forgotten' as an intrinsic part of the Right to Privacy under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling came in a case involving the removal of outdated personal information from public digital platforms that was causing reputational harm to an individual.
• The Court urged the government to expedite the enactment of a robust, comprehensive data protection law that addresses emerging challenges of the digital era, including data localization and cross-border data flows.
• It highlighted the need for a balance between an individual's right to privacy and the public's right to information, especially concerning public figures.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for internet intermediaries, search engines, and social media platforms operating in India.

• Discussions focused on joint naval exercises, information sharing, capacity building for coastal surveillance, and combating piracy and illegal fishing.
• Both sides reiterated commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, upholding international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• The dialogue also touched upon humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) operations and enhancing interoperability among maritime agencies.
• This engagement underscores India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic convergence with ASEAN nations.

GS2.explainer:

• The address highlighted the anachronistic nature of the current UNSC structure, which does not reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• India emphasized the need for greater representation for developing countries and increased transparency in the Council's functioning.
• The statement also touched upon global challenges such as climate change, terrorism, and sustainable development, urging collective action.
• This consistent advocacy underscores India's aspiration for a permanent seat in a reformed UNSC.

GS2.headline: Global Hunger Index 2024: India's Position and Efforts to Combat Malnutrition
GS2.explainer:
• The Global Hunger Index (GHI) 2024 report was released, indicating India's continued progress in addressing hunger and malnutrition, though challenges persist.
• India's ranking improved slightly, reflecting ongoing efforts through various government schemes targeting food security and nutritional outcomes.
• The report highlighted specific areas where India needs to intensify efforts, such as reducing child wasting and improving dietary diversity.
• Government officials reiterated their commitment to achieving Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 2: Zero Hunger by 2030.
• The GHI report serves as a critical tool for assessing national and global progress in combating hunger and identifying areas for intervention.

GS3.headline: India Unveils New Policy Framework to Accelerate Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) announced a comprehensive policy framework offering production-linked incentives (PLI) and viability gap funding (VGF) for green hydrogen and electrolyser manufacturing.
• The policy aims to reduce the cost of green hydrogen production, making it competitive with fossil-fuel-based hydrogen, and promote India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
• Key components include tax breaks for early adopters, dedicated green hydrogen corridors, and R&D support for advanced electrolyser technologies.
• The move is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and achieving Net Zero emissions by 2070.
• Industry experts laud the policy for providing long-term visibility and investor confidence in the nascent green hydrogen sector.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
New and renewable energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
MNRE is the nodal ministry.
Target: 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
PLI and VGF are financial incentives.
Aim to reduce green hydrogen cost to $1/kg.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy for energy security and decarbonization through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic implications of the new policy for domestic manufacturing and exports.
Evaluate the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine the role of policy support in fostering new-age industries and achieving climate targets.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with electrolysers and renewable energy sources.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: RBI Expands Digital Rupee (e₹) Pilot to Include More Banks and Use Cases
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced a significant expansion of its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot project for both retail (e₹-R) and wholesale (e₹-W) segments.
• The expansion includes onboarding more public and private sector banks and exploring new use cases beyond interbank settlements and person-to-person transactions.
• Focus areas for new use cases include programmable payments, offline functionality, and integration with existing payment infrastructure like UPI.
• The RBI aims to gather more data on user experience, technological robustness, and economic impact before a full-scale rollout.
• This move signals India's proactive approach to digital currencies and financial innovation, potentially revolutionizing the payment landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: RBI launched the wholesale CBDC pilot on Nov 1, 2022.
Retail CBDC pilot launched on Dec 1, 2022.
CBDC is a legal tender issued by a central bank in digital form.
It is different from cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin.
Programmable payments allow for specific conditions for transactions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and risks associated with the introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency in India.
Analyze how the Digital Rupee could impact India's financial ecosystem, including banks and payment service providers.
Examine the technological challenges and regulatory considerations in developing and deploying a national CBDC.
Compare and contrast India's approach to CBDC with that of other major economies globally.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital representation of Indian Rupee symbol with binary code background.
GS3.trivia: The Bahamas was the first country to launch a full-fledged retail CBDC, known as the 'Sand Dollar', in 2020.

GS3.headline: ISRO Achieves Key Milestone in Next-Gen Launch Vehicle Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a critical hot test of the cryogenic upper stage engine for its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV).
• The NGLV is envisioned as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle capable of carrying heavier payloads into various orbits.
• This test validates the indigenous design and manufacturing capabilities for advanced propulsion systems, crucial for future space missions.
• The NGLV aims to replace existing operational launchers like PSLV and GSLV, offering enhanced payload capacity and reduced launch costs through reusability.
• This development is vital for India's ambitions in commercial space launches, human spaceflight (Gaganyaan), and deep-space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: PSLV is ISRO's workhorse launch vehicle.
GSLV is used for heavier satellites and geostationary orbits.
Cryogenic engines use liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen as propellants.
RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator) is a precursor project.
NGLV aims for a payload capacity of up to 10 tonnes to GTO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing indigenous heavy-lift and reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program.
Analyze how NGLV can enhance India's capabilities in commercial space, human spaceflight, and deep-space missions.
Examine the economic benefits and technological challenges associated with reusable launch vehicles.
Evaluate the role of ISRO in promoting scientific temper and technological self-reliance in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Diagram of the Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) with a focus on its engine.
GS3.trivia: The first successful flight of a cryogenic engine by ISRO was in 2014 with the GSLV-D5 mission.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches New Wetland Conservation and Management Plan
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) unveiled an enhanced national action plan for the conservation and sustainable management of wetlands.
• The plan focuses on holistic approaches, including community participation, scientific inventorying of wetlands, restoration of degraded sites, and stricter enforcement against encroachment.
• It integrates climate change resilience measures and promotes eco-tourism opportunities around identified wetlands.
• Special emphasis is laid on wetlands outside the Ramsar List, ensuring broader protection for diverse aquatic ecosystems.
• This initiative is critical for biodiversity preservation, water security, and mitigating the impacts of climate change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Ramsar Convention signed in 1971 in Ramsar, Iran.
World Wetlands Day is celebrated on February 2nd.
Wetlands are crucial for flood control, water purification, and biodiversity.
India has a significant number of Ramsar Sites.
Wetland (Conservation and Management) Rules, 2017 are in place.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze the key features of the new wetland conservation plan and its potential effectiveness.
Examine the role of community participation and inter-sectoral coordination in wetland management.
Suggest policy measures to enhance the protection of wetlands beyond designated Ramsar Sites.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
GS3.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha was the first Indian wetland of international importance designated under the Ramsar Convention.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Releases New 'Positive Indigenisation List' for Defence Procurement
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence released its fourth 'Positive Indigenisation List' (PIL) comprising 101 items that will be procured only from domestic sources after specific timelines.
• This list includes complex systems, sub-systems, and components, further boosting India's self-reliance in defence manufacturing.
• The move is aimed at reducing import dependency, stimulating domestic R&D, and strengthening the 'Make in India' initiative in the defence sector.
• The policy provides a clear roadmap for the Indian defence industry to invest in technological upgrades and production capabilities.
• It is expected to create significant opportunities for MSMEs and startups in the defence ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.

DAP 2020 promotes indigenous defence production.

The goal is to achieve $5 billion in defence exports by 2025.
MSMEs play a crucial role in defence supply chains.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of defence indigenization for India's national security and economic growth.
Analyze how the 'Positive Indigenisation List' contributes to achieving self-reliance in the defence sector.
Examine the challenges faced by the domestic defence industry and suggest measures to overcome them.
Evaluate the role of government policies in fostering a robust defence manufacturing ecosystem in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Modern defence equipment manufactured in India.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, a trend the indigenization drive aims to reverse.

GS3.headline: UNEP Report Highlights Persistent Global Fossil Fuel Subsidies Despite Climate Goals
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the UN Environment Programme (UNEP) revealed that global fossil fuel subsidies continue to rise, undermining efforts to combat climate change.
• The report indicates that governments worldwide spent record amounts on supporting fossil fuels in 2023, largely due to energy price volatility and cost-of-living crises.
• It calls for an urgent phase-out of inefficient fossil fuel subsidies and a redirection of funds towards renewable energy and sustainable alternatives.
• The persistence of these subsidies creates market distortions, discourages green investments, and exacerbates greenhouse gas emissions.
• This finding poses a significant challenge for countries committed to their Paris Agreement targets and achieving global decarbonization.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: UNEP is the leading global environmental authority.
Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2°C.
Fossil fuels include coal, oil, and natural gas.
Subsidies can be direct financial transfers or tax breaks.
IPCC (Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change) provides scientific assessments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic and environmental implications of persistent fossil fuel subsidies globally.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in phasing out fossil fuel subsidies while ensuring energy access and affordability.
Examine the strategies for a 'just energy transition' that supports vulnerable populations during the shift to renewable energy.
Evaluate the role of international organizations like UNEP in advocating for sustainable energy policies.
GS3.imageDescription: A graph showing rising fossil fuel subsidies alongside a backdrop of industrial emissions.
GS3.trivia: The G20 nations committed to phasing out inefficient fossil fuel subsidies in 2009, a pledge that has seen limited progress.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Shows Strong Recovery, Focus on Sustainable Tourism
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism reported a significant rebound in both domestic and international tourist arrivals, surpassing pre-pandemic levels in several key destinations.
• The recovery is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh', improved connectivity, and targeted marketing campaigns.
• There's a growing emphasis on promoting sustainable and responsible tourism, including eco-tourism, rural tourism, and cultural heritage circuits.
• New policies are being formulated to enhance infrastructure, ensure tourist safety, and develop lesser-known destinations.
• The sector is poised for further growth, contributing significantly to employment generation and foreign exchange earnings.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

Incredible India campaign targets international tourists.
Tourism sector is a major employer.
Foreign Exchange Earnings (FEEs) from tourism are a key indicator.
Swadesh Darshan Scheme focuses on theme-based tourist circuits.

Analyze the concept of sustainable tourism and its importance for India's diverse ecological and cultural heritage.
Examine government initiatives aimed at promoting tourism and their effectiveness.
Suggest strategies to further boost tourism, enhance tourist experience, and ensure local community benefits.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant picture of a popular Indian tourist destination, showcasing its cultural richness.
GS3.trivia: India's first National Tourism Policy was announced in 1982.

GS3.headline: India Pushes for Ethical AI Governance Framework at Global AI Summit
GS3.explainer:
• India participated in a global Artificial Intelligence (AI) summit, advocating for a multilateral governance framework that ensures ethical, responsible, and inclusive development and deployment of AI.
• The Indian delegation highlighted the need for a human-centric approach to AI, addressing concerns around bias, privacy, accountability, and the socio-economic impact of automation.
• India emphasized its commitment to leveraging AI for public good, citing initiatives in healthcare, agriculture, and education.
• The discussions also focused on international cooperation for AI research, standard-setting, and capacity building, especially for developing nations.
• This stance aligns with India's vision of 'AI for All' and its growing leadership in digital public infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog released 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence' in 2018.
India's G20 presidency focused on Digital Public Infrastructure.
AI can be applied in various sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education.
Concerns include algorithmic bias and job displacement.
Responsible AI development is crucial.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges and societal implications of rapidly advancing Artificial Intelligence technologies.
Analyze India's approach to AI governance and its advocacy for a human-centric global framework.
Examine the potential of AI to drive socio-economic development in India, particularly in priority sectors.
Evaluate the need for international cooperation and standard-setting in regulating AI to prevent misuse and ensure equitable benefits.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized brain connecting with digital circuits, representing AI and its global reach.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference.

GS3.headline: Editorial: Towards Resilient Urban Planning for Climate Change Adaptation
GS3.explainer:
• This editorial highlights the increasing vulnerability of Indian cities to extreme weather events, calling for a paradigm shift in urban planning.
• It emphasizes the need for integrated strategies that incorporate climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction into city development plans.
• Key recommendations include investing in green infrastructure, improving drainage systems, promoting sustainable transport, and strengthening early warning mechanisms.
• The editorial argues for greater collaboration between urban local bodies, state governments, and citizens in building climate-resilient cities.
• It underscores that sustainable urban development is not just an environmental imperative but also an economic necessity for India's future.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted challenges faced by Indian cities due to climate change and rapid urbanization.
Discuss the principles of resilient urban planning and how they can be integrated into India's urban development policies.
Examine the role of green infrastructure, technological solutions, and community engagement in building climate-resilient cities.
Suggest policy recommendations for strengthening urban local bodies and fostering inter-agency coordination for effective disaster management.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern cityscape with elements of green spaces and flood-resistant architecture.
GS3.trivia: Over 35% of India's population lives in urban areas, a figure projected to reach 50% by 2050, making urban resilience critical.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Scheme to Boost Digitalization of MSMEs
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) launched a new scheme aimed at facilitating the digitalization and technological upgrade of MSMEs across various sectors.
• The scheme offers financial assistance for adopting digital tools, cloud computing, e-commerce integration, and cybersecurity solutions.
• It seeks to enhance the competitiveness of Indian MSMEs in global markets and integrate them into the digital economy.
• Capacity building programs and workshops will also be conducted to improve digital literacy among MSME entrepreneurs.
• This initiative is expected to drive innovation, improve efficiency, and create new employment opportunities within the MSME sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

Udyam Registration is a government portal for MSMEs.
Mudra Yojana provides loans to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises.

Cybersecurity is a growing concern for businesses.

Examine the role of government schemes and policy support in fostering digital adoption among small businesses.
Suggest measures to overcome barriers to digitalization, such as lack of digital literacy and access to affordable technology.

GS3.trivia: The MSME sector is estimated to contribute about 30% to India's GDP and 48% to its exports.

GS3.headline: New Study Highlights Unique Biodiversity and Threats in Eastern Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A recent ecological study published in a leading journal underscored the unique biodiversity of the Eastern Ghats, identifying several endemic species of flora and fauna.
• The study warned of increasing threats to this mountain range, including deforestation, mining, agricultural expansion, and climate change.
• Researchers emphasized the critical role of the Eastern Ghats as a crucial ecological corridor and a biodiversity hotspot, often overshadowed by the Western Ghats.
• It called for urgent conservation efforts, including designating new protected areas and implementing sustainable land-use practices.
• The findings are vital for formulating targeted conservation policies and promoting ecological restoration in the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.

They are older than the Western Ghats.
They receive rainfall from the Northeast Monsoon.

Nallamala Hills, Palkonda Range are parts of Eastern Ghats.

Analyze the major threats to the biodiversity of the Eastern Ghats and their long-term environmental implications.
Examine the role of scientific research in informing conservation strategies and policy decisions.
Suggest comprehensive measures for the conservation and sustainable management of the Eastern Ghats' ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Eastern Ghats with a focus on its diverse flora.
GS3.trivia: Mahendragiri in Odisha is one of the highest peaks in the Eastern Ghats.

GS3.headline: India's Startup Ecosystem Continues Robust Growth Amidst Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by industry body NASSCOM highlighted the continued robust growth of India's startup ecosystem, with a significant increase in new ventures and funding.
• Despite global economic headwinds, India remains a top destination for venture capital, particularly in sectors like FinTech, HealthTech, and DeepTech.
• Government initiatives such as 'Startup India' and 'Fund of Funds for Startups' are credited for fostering a conducive environment for innovation.
• The report also pointed to the increasing number of unicorns and the expansion of startup activity beyond metropolitan areas to Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
• This growth is crucial for job creation, technological innovation, and India's aspiration to become a $5 trillion economy.

Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

India has the third-largest startup ecosystem globally.
Government provides tax benefits and easier compliance for startups.

Suggest measures to further strengthen the startup ecosystem and ensure its equitable spread across different regions and sectors.
GS3.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space.
GS3.trivia: India added a record number of unicorns in 2021, cementing its position as a global startup powerhouse.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Frog in Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The newly identified amphibian, named 'Nyctibatrachus sahyadriensis', belongs to the genus of night frogs, known for their unique breeding habits.
• This discovery underscores the unparalleled biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect the fragile habitats of such endemic species from climate change and human encroachment.
• The finding contributes significantly to the understanding of amphibian evolution and distribution in the Indian subcontinent.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
They are one of the 8 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity in the world.

Nyctibatrachus is a genus of frogs endemic to the Western Ghats.

Suggest comprehensive strategies for the protection and sustainable management of the Western Ghats ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of the newly discovered frog species in its natural habitat.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and influence India's monsoon weather patterns.

GS3.headline: CERT-In Issues Advisory on Rising Cyber Threats and Data Breaches
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) issued a comprehensive advisory warning against a surge in cybersecurity threats, including ransomware attacks and data breaches.
• The advisory urged government agencies, critical infrastructure operators, and private organizations to bolster their digital defenses and implement robust cybersecurity protocols.
• It highlighted the growing sophistication of cyber adversaries and the increasing targeting of personal data and financial systems.
• CERT-In recommended regular security audits, employee training, and timely patching of vulnerabilities to mitigate risks.
• This alert underscores the critical importance of national cybersecurity strategies in safeguarding digital assets and citizen data.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.

Examine the challenges in securing critical infrastructure and citizen data from sophisticated cyberattacks.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including legislative, technological, and public awareness initiatives, to enhance India's cybersecurity posture.
